tipRules.tip: KVD: 'Drop-shot excels over muddy, mucky, slimy bottoms — suspends the bait above gunk. Favor it for vertical fishing under the boat, video gaming on graph. Heavier weight for fast drop without impacting bait action — good for heavy current too.'

KVD: 'Neko rig is excellent on gunky bottoms — hook above weight stays clean in the strike zone. Drop it vertical under the boat or cast out around. Use lead nail weights and cut excess to perfect the fall. 3/32oz tungsten for deep.'

KVD: 'Shaky head is my choice for casting to specific targets — bushes, seawalls, riprap, standing timber, pilings, docks. Best over clean hard bottom (sand, gravel, rock) and generally 15 feet or less for me.'
KVD: 'Ned rig is a huge player on clear water lakes with clean rocky bottoms and mixed species. Absolute deal in goby-infested Great Lakes waters. Great cleanup bait for smallmouth.'

knowledge.insight: KVD: 'For all four finesse techniques (drop-shot, Neko, shaky head, Ned): 10lb braid to 8lb fluorocarbon leader with FG knot as baseline. Go heavier for cover or colored water. Longer 7-2 to 7-4 rods for open water long casts, shorter 6-10 to 7-foot for tight target work.'

KVD: 'After scouring a productive spot with power approaches, drop down to lighter line and finesse to pick up the scraps. Cleaning up with finesse has become a regular part of my game — these techniques revitalize a worn-out fishing spot.'
